stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of water flow rate using a flow meter. The measurements have a mean of 102.8 L/min and a standard deviation of 9.4 L/min. However, calibration tests reveal that a flow meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.4% and 7.4%. Estimate the true water flow rate and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 102.8 L/min. After correcting for the 5.9% bias: ≈ 109.2 L/min. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.4%-7.4%) and sampling variability: [104.1, 114.4]. Point estimate: 109.2 L/min.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
